title: Estimated Population in the Palestine Mid-Year by Governorate, 1997-2026
pages_or_sections: table rows for West Bank, Gaza Strip, and governorates
strengths: Direct subnational population baselines by governorate; useful for episode mapping and denominator ceilings.
limitations: Governorate-scale only; not sufficient on its own for camp, corridor, or displacement-adjusted exposure denominators.
notes: Useful for West Bank/Gaza episode mapping, but Jenin camp full estimate relies on camp-specific sources rather than governorate totals.

citation_short: OCHA Special Focus on casualties and context
title: OCHA Special Focus: Israeli-Palestinian Conflict Casualties and Context
author_or_org: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs, occupied Palestinian territory
strengths: Clear by-side victim-status shares and clear location/mode contrasts across Israel, West Bank, and Gaza.
limitations: Published after the episode; not a microdata registry and not camp-specific.
notes: Primary use here is episode splitting and conflict-family context, not Jenin-specific numerators.

title: Jenin: IDF Military Operations
pages_or_sections: Summary and sections on battle chronology, casualties, and camp population
geographic_scope: Jenin refugee camp and immediate environs
date_scope: Battle of 3-11 April 2002, with field research 19-28 April 2002
strengths: Best single source for Jenin status-coded Palestinian deaths; explicit split between civilians, suspected armed Palestinians, and unresolved cases.
limitations: Militant headcount is only given as media-estimate context; not a full named-victim microdataset release.
notes: Core numerator source for the fully estimated Jenin episode.

citation_short: UN Secretary-General report on Jenin and other cities
title: Report of the Secretary-General prepared pursuant to General Assembly resolution ES-10/10
geographic_scope: Jenin and other West Bank cities during Operation Defensive Shield
date_scope: Approximately beginning of March to 7 May 2002
strengths: Useful cross-check on Jenin death toll, displacement, battle timing, and Israeli military fatalities.
limitations: Not based on an on-site UN fact-finding visit; mixes Israeli public statements, Palestinian submissions, and NGO material.
notes: Used here mainly for displacement, timing, Israeli military deaths, and upper-bound militant exposure wording.

citation_short: Washington Institute on Operation Defensive Shield
title: Operation Defensive Shield: The Israeli Actions in the West Bank
author_or_org: The Washington Institute for Near East Policy
pages_or_sections: Aims and Tactics; Results of the Operation
strengths: Useful for why 2002 should be separated as a multi-city urban-incursion episode with regular/reserve force rotation.
limitations: Policy-analysis perspective, not a casualty registry or neutral denominator source.
notes: Used for episode design and operational-rotation context rather than direct numerators.

title: Trigger Happy: Unjustified Shooting and the Open-Fire Regulations
pages_or_sections: p. 2 and related discussion of open-fire policy
strengths: Contemporary rights report with early-intifada fatality totals and victim-status context.
limitations: Not designed as an episode-specific DR source; broad territorial rollup rather than locality-specific microdata.
notes: Useful for opening-phase context and for showing why early West Bank/Gaza episodes need separate treatment.

title: 2002 Activity Report
strengths: Useful snapshot of status-segmented deaths and child fatalities during the 2002 escalation.
limitations: Annual-report format; the cited Israeli summary line uses the word 'casualties', so it is treated as context unless cross-checked elsewhere.
notes: Supports the case for a distinct 2002 peak phase and provides auxiliary child-death evidence, but the casualty wording is not used as a direct-death numerator here.

title: Erased in a Moment: Suicide Bombing Attacks Against Israeli Civilians
geographic_scope: Israel and selected occupied-territory target sites
strengths: Clear source for the inside-Israel/settlement bombing episode and for civilian-targeting mode.
limitations: Victim-focused; not useful for Palestinian civilian or combatant exposure denominators.
notes: Important for keeping the bombing theater distinct from West Bank and Gaza battle zones.

title: An Empirical Analysis of Fatalities in the Palestinian-Israeli Conflict
author_or_org: David A. Jaeger and M. Daniele Paserman
strengths: Useful for broad time segmentation and overall tempo of fatalities during the Second Intifada.
limitations: Not a subnational denominator source; its status categories are not sufficient for strict episode-level DR without further reconciliation.
notes: Used here to reinforce temporal anchors, not as a direct numerator for the Jenin DR.
